div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, as well present as future, Walter son of Alan, steward of the king of the Scots, sends greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of Saint Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, for the sake of the souls of my lords, David and Malcolm the kings, and of Earl Henry, and of my father and mother, and of all my ancestors and successors, and for the salvation of William king of Scots and David his brother, and for the salvation of my own soul and of my heirs' souls, in free and pure and everlasting alms, the whole land of Mauchline by the boundaries which Donald and Gilla Brigte at my instruction perambulated between the land of Mauchline and the land of Gilbert son of Richer, to wit, from the place where 'Mulneburne' falls into the Ayr below 'Culteicouan', and going up by the same burn as far as 'Fulelech', and so going upwards by the same syke, and across the bog as far as the burn which descends from 'Rederburne' to the burn which falls into 'Duueloch', and by the burn which comes down from 'Duueloch', descending by the same burn into 'Saxnoc', and going up by 'Saxnoc' to 'Polmulin', and thus ascending by 'Polmulin' up to the midst of 'Acthenebron', and from there upwards as far as the ridge and so by the ridge as the water flows across them from the south side to the head of the Nith, and thus downwards by the Nith as far as the Ayr, and by the Ayr downwards to the Lugar, and by the Lugar upwards to the 'Dufpol', and by the 'Dufpol' upwards by the boundaries which I caused them to perambulate, as far as the other side of 'Monecapel'. With this land I have given to them the open lands and haughs on the north side of the river Ayr which they might want to cultivate, and a fishery at the mouth of the river Ayr, with one net at first, to wit, of the three nets; and in addition that they may use the river Ayr as well for fishing as for other things as might please and benefit them; and just as I myself have well had this alms for my own use, I will that the aforesaid monks of Melrose as freely, quitly and honourably should hold and possess as a perpetual holding from all services and pleas of assize also and aids and every secular exaction and custom for ever, just as freely and quitly and honourably as any alms can be held and possessed. In addition, I have given and granted to them the whole pasture of my forest up to the boundaries of Douglas and Lesmahagow and Glengavel on the north side of the river Ayr, with all easements, in wood and in all things necessary for them, and one ploughgate of land for cultivating in those places where

And be it known that I and my heirs shall do forinsec service for our lord the king for all the aforesaid, and we shall warrant to them the aforesaid boundaries and whatever is in them, in wood and in plain, and in all other things, against all men for ever, as free and pure and perpetual alms. And because I have willed that this my alms should be strong and established nor by any means infringed, my lord King William has at my request confirmed all the things aforesaid by his charter, just as his own alms. Witness Alan my son, Liulf son of Master Robert of Côtentin, Robert of Montgomery, Robert son of Fulbert, Robert son of Maias, Ralph of Kent, Walter Côtentin, Richard the clerk, Richard Wallce, Adam of Newton.
